jokes.text: I have a favour... - Oh, great. Um... I would love a ride to work. - I meant, I have a favour to ask.
jokes.setup: Moira starts to ask a favor, but Alexis interrupts thinking she's being offered one
jokes.punchline: Moira has to clarify the direction of the favor request
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on 'having a favor' - shows Alexis's self-centeredness efficiently

jokes.text: Well, who says that, 'I have a favour for you?'
jokes.setup: Alexis defending her interpretation of Moira's phrasing
jokes.punchline: She's genuinely confused by normal phrasing because she's self-centered
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Alexis genuinely doesn't understand selfless phrasing

jokes.text: We're all pitching in these days, dear. Like communists or non-union actors.
jokes.setup: Moira explaining why everyone must work
jokes.punchline: Compares family cooperation to communists and non-union actors
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira - unexpected comparison that's both pretentious and weirdly accurate

jokes.text: I didn't tell you because I don't want you to come.
jokes.setup: Family asking about graduation
jokes.punchline: Alexis bluntly admits she doesn't want them there
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest moment that's both funny and sad - typical family dysfunction

jokes.text: It's bad enough that I have to wear that unflattering black dress and then the hat with the thing on it in front of people that I don't know.
jokes.setup: Explaining why she doesn't want family at graduation
jokes.punchline: Describes graduation attire like it's a fashion nightmare
jokes.explanation: Alexis reducing graduation ceremony to fashion concerns - perfectly in character

jokes.text: I was at your first graduation and it's not my fault that you weren't there.
jokes.setup: Alexis saying she doesn't want family at graduation
jokes.punchline: Moira reveals Alexis missed her own first graduation
jokes.explanation: Dark but hilarious - reveals past irresponsibility while deflecting current guilt

jokes.text: It's my birthday.
jokes.setup: Family saying everyone has to go to graduation unless there's a special excuse
jokes.punchline: David's flat delivery of his birthday as the excuse
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan moment - David's resignation to being forgotten

jokes.text: We, we didn't forget. - No, we've been talking about it for weeks. - You've been talking about it for weeks.
jokes.setup: Parents trying to cover for forgetting David's birthday
jokes.punchline: David immediately calls out that only Moira was talking about it
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the lie falling apart under scrutiny

jokes.text: I'm very uninterested in that option. - As am I.
jokes.setup: Parents suggesting combined birthday/graduation celebration
jokes.punchline: Both siblings immediately and formally reject the idea
jokes.explanation: Sibling unity in rejecting their parents' attempts at celebration

jokes.text: They can't say we didn't try.
jokes.setup: Both children have rejected any family involvement in their milestones
jokes.punchline: Johnny's resigned acceptance of minimal parental effort
jokes.explanation: Johnny's defeated dad energy - technically true but hilariously low bar

jokes.text: Ugh. - What? - Woof... David.
jokes.setup: Alexis looking at David
jokes.punchline: Her disgusted reaction to David's appearance
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag - Alexis being Alexis about David's fashion choices

jokes.text: Oh. Well, isn't that thoughtful. I wish everyone remembered special days like that, but, alas, that's not what this world is anymore.
jokes.setup: Customer asking for gift receipt for anniversary
jokes.punchline: Moira's dramatic lament about forgotten special days while forgetting her son's birthday
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony - Moira being hypocritical about remembering special days

jokes.text: Here is a question. Um, has your family ever forgotten your birthday? Like your parents and your sister, collectively, as a whole?
jokes.setup: David asking Patrick about family dynamics
jokes.punchline: Overly specific question reveals his current situation
jokes.explanation: David's attempt at casual inquiry about very specific situation

jokes.text: That would be a no. No, we've always had some kind of party. Oh. - In fact, sometimes two parties.
jokes.setup: David asking if Patrick's family ever forgot his birthday
jokes.punchline: Patrick not only never forgotten but sometimes had multiple parties, making David's situation worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation making David feel worse about his situation

jokes.text: Ah, I plan on popping a pill, crying a bit and falling asleep early. So just a regular weeknight.
jokes.setup: Patrick asking about David's birthday plans
jokes.punchline: David's dark description that's apparently his normal routine
jokes.explanation: Dark humor revealing David's depression through his casual delivery

jokes.text: I've heard people raving about how 'moderately edible' the food is there.
jokes.setup: Patrick suggesting dinner at local cafe
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about the restaurant's quality
jokes.explanation: Good small-town humor about low dining expectations

jokes.text: Those are wet now. - That's a lot of spritzing. - I'm gonna dry those off.
jokes.setup: Someone apparently over-spritzing plants in the store
jokes.punchline: The awkward acknowledgment of the over-watering
jokes.explanation: Minor visual gag - competent but unremarkable

jokes.text: formal training is usually encouraged. - Well, I don't have formal training.
jokes.setup: Ted explaining why other applicants are qualified
jokes.punchline: Alexis stating the obvious about her own lack of qualifications
jokes.explanation: Simple character moment showing Alexis's self-awareness gap

jokes.text: No. God no.
jokes.setup: Alexis asking if she should be assisting with surgeries
jokes.punchline: Ted's immediate and emphatic rejection of the idea
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat - Ted's horror at the thought of Alexis doing surgery

jokes.text: The poor thing went to cat camp.
jokes.setup: Reading resume of qualified applicant
jokes.punchline: Alexis's dismissive description of specialized training as 'cat camp'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis moment - reducing professional training to something silly

jokes.text: Maybe there's a job out there that I'm better suited for. And some other like gorgeous, slightly under qualified girl is sitting at my desk asking herself the same question.
jokes.setup: Alexis realizing she might not be right for the job
jokes.punchline: Her assumption that her replacement would also be gorgeous and under-qualified
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work - Alexis assumes all women in her position are gorgeous and unqualified

jokes.text: I'm just realizing that wanting to come in and laugh at your animal puns isn't enough when there is like people here.
jokes.setup: Alexis explaining why she's not qualified
jokes.punchline: Revealing her main job motivation was laughing at Ted's puns
jokes.explanation: Good character moment revealing Alexis's shallow work motivation

jokes.text: Those were genuine laughs? - Sometimes, yeah.
jokes.setup: Ted learning Alexis was actually laughing at his puns
jokes.punchline: Alexis's qualified admission that only some laughs were real
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Ted's pride deflated and Alexis's honesty

jokes.text: Well, I can't say the reception will be as fun, but that's really generous of you, Alexis. Like, surprisingly generous.
jokes.setup: Ted commenting on Alexis quitting
jokes.punchline: His backhanded compliment about her being 'surprisingly generous'
jokes.explanation: Ted's gentle roast of Alexis's usual selfishness

jokes.text: Have you tried restarting it? - What? - Oh. Sorry, I thought you were having trouble with the computer again.
jokes.setup: Johnny calling for Stevie excitedly
jokes.punchline: Stevie assumes it's another tech problem
jokes.explanation: Good running gag about Johnny's tech incompetence

jokes.text: Are you sure you didn't put the same reservation in twice? Because you've done that before.
jokes.setup: Johnny excited about being sold out
jokes.punchline: Stevie's deflating reality check about his competence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie deflation of Johnny's enthusiasm with past evidence

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, that burnt out years ago. Sadly it hasn't been an issue, but... Maybe we could use the 'no' from the 'no refunds' sign.
jokes.setup: Johnny wanting to light up the 'no vacancy' sign
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's broken and suggesting cannibaling other signs
jokes.explanation: Good detail comedy about the motel's sad state

jokes.text: I don't think we're those kind of people. - In the sky! Here we go! Yeah! Big day!
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to get Stevie to high-five
jokes.punchline: Stevie's flat rejection while Johnny celebrates alone
jokes.explanation: Classic dynamic - Johnny's enthusiasm vs Stevie's deadpan resistance

jokes.text: Because we are an ensemble group And this requires a lead vocal that just happens to be in your range.
jokes.setup: Moira wanting to do a solo number
jokes.punchline: Ronnie pointing out Moira's transparent self-serving song choice
jokes.explanation: Good character conflict - Ronnie calling out Moira's diva behavior

jokes.text: when one of us shines, all of us shine. That is the meaning of ensemble.
jokes.setup: Ronnie criticizing Moira's solo ambitions
jokes.punchline: Moira's self-serving interpretation of ensemble work
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira logic - making selfishness sound noble

jokes.text: Oh. Is the bad news that there is no good news? My aunt used to play this game with me a lot.
jokes.setup: Someone mentioning good and bad news
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn's childhood trauma about fake good news
jokes.explanation: Dark family humor delivered with Jocelyn's innocent tone

jokes.text: They got a terrible stomach flu that just run right through them.
jokes.setup: Explaining why another group dropped out
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn's graphic description of the illness
jokes.explanation: Simple gross-out humor - functional but unremarkable

jokes.text: Alexis has veritably browbeaten us not to attend.
jokes.setup: Explaining why they won't be at graduation
jokes.punchline: Moira's dramatic vocabulary to describe Alexis being direct
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira - over-the-top vocabulary for simple situations

jokes.text: I wouldn't be surprised if she skips the whole affair herself. Waggish little nymph, that she is.
jokes.setup: Continuing about Alexis's attitude toward graduation
jokes.punchline: Moira's elaborate description of Alexis as a 'waggish little nymph'
jokes.explanation: Peak Moira vocabulary - turning Alexis into a mythical creature

jokes.text: I'd advise against the mortarboard. It'll flattens the hair and pull focus away from that beautiful face.
jokes.setup: Alexis asking about graduation attire
jokes.punchline: Moira treating graduation cap like a fashion choice
jokes.explanation: Good Moira moment - prioritizing appearance over ceremony

jokes.text: But by not wearing one, you'll stand out. And by the fact that you're a good ten years older than everyone else.
jokes.setup: Advising Alexis to skip the graduation cap
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning Alexis's age difference from classmates
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor - Moira's tactless honesty about Alexis's situation

jokes.text: I don't know why. Everybody graduates from high school.
jokes.setup: Moira saying she's proud of Alexis
jokes.punchline: Alexis minimizing an achievement she actually struggled with
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Alexis's defensive minimizing of her achievement

jokes.text: It's not too late if you wanna come. - Hey, it's okay. Don't worry about me. Mommy is going to be fine.
jokes.setup: Alexis softening and inviting Moira to graduation
jokes.punchline: Moira's defensive response and referring to herself as 'Mommy'
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamics - both trying to save face while wanting connection

jokes.text: you have been and always will be one of my top priorities.
jokes.setup: Moira trying to reassure Alexis of her importance
jokes.punchline: The qualifier 'one of my top priorities' instead of 'my top priority'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira moment - trying to be loving but can't commit fully

jokes.text: I almost didn't see you there through the mad rush of the Friday night dinner crowd.
jokes.setup: Patrick arriving at empty restaurant
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic comment about the non-existent crowd
jokes.explanation: Standard small-town restaurant humor - competent but predictable

jokes.text: I assumed that the cafe would have a dinner jacket policy and I didn't wanna wear one of theirs.
jokes.setup: Patrick complimenting David's appearance
jokes.punchline: David's assumption about dress codes at a small-town diner
jokes.explanation: Good David character work - his fish-out-of-water assumptions about rural dining

jokes.text: they've done such a wonderful job of editing down the menu to a very concise, world-class selection of international cuisine.
jokes.setup: David describing the restaurant menu
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top description of what's probably a basic diner menu
jokes.explanation: Classic David - making small-town mediocrity sound sophisticated

jokes.text: In case you didn't know, I'm widely popular. Some might even venture to call me beloved.
jokes.setup: Explaining why only two people came to his birthday dinner
jokes.punchline: David's delusional self-assessment of his popularity
jokes.explanation: Great David delusion - claiming popularity while evidence suggests otherwise

jokes.text: So I think I'm crashing a date?
jokes.setup: Stevie realizing the birthday dinner setup
jokes.punchline: Her blunt assessment of the romantic situation
jokes.explanation: Good Stevie moment - cutting through romantic tension with directness

jokes.text: It's very nicely wrapped. So I think I'm crashing a date? - He thinks you guys were here, one-on-one. He brought you a present. I didn't even get you a present.
jokes.setup: Stevie analyzing the romantic situation
jokes.punchline: Her realization that she's the odd one out without a gift
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Stevie's awkward realization

jokes.text: if there's anything remotely sentimental in here, he is on a date with you right now.
jokes.setup: Stevie looking at Patrick's gift
jokes.punchline: Her blunt romantic analysis based on gift sentimentality
jokes.explanation: Good Stevie logic - reducing romance to gift analysis

jokes.text: So you put it on the table? - Yeah.
jokes.setup: Patrick asking about the gift placement logic
jokes.punchline: Stevie's matter-of-fact confirmation of illogical behavior
jokes.explanation: Minor awkward moment - functional but unremarkable

jokes.text: This is the first gift I haven't bought myself in a very long time. So thank you.
jokes.setup: David receiving Patrick's gift
jokes.punchline: Revealing his sad gift-receiving history
jokes.explanation: Touching and funny - reveals David's loneliness while showing gratitude

jokes.text: Oh, it's just the receipt from our first sale at the store.
jokes.setup: David opening Patrick's gift
jokes.punchline: The simple but meaningful nature of the gift
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment - simple but deeply meaningful gift choice

jokes.text: Pretty sure I scraped off all the freezer burn.
jokes.setup: Serving mozzarella sticks
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of food quality issues
jokes.explanation: Standard small-town restaurant humor about food quality

jokes.text: these don't travel well. Even coming from the kitchen they lost a lot of shape.
jokes.setup: Warning about takeout food
jokes.punchline: Admitting the food barely survives being served
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the food quality joke

jokes.text: Let's try to find another word for 'quit.' You know, it kinda implies poor management.
jokes.setup: Alexis saying she quit the vet job
jokes.punchline: Ted's ego-protecting reframing of the situation
jokes.explanation: Good Ted character moment - his professional insecurity showing

jokes.text: You're Damien Steele? - Shh! Come on, I don't want the whole town to find out.
jokes.setup: Johnny realizing Roland is his mystery guest
jokes.punchline: Roland's paranoia about his fake identity being discovered
jokes.explanation: Good Roland absurdity - treating transparent alias like witness protection

jokes.text: the mayor of the town can't use his real name to check into a love motel. - Yeah, it's not a love motel, Roland.
jokes.setup: Roland explaining his fake name
jokes.punchline: Johnny's defensive correction about his motel's reputation
jokes.explanation: Good character conflict - Johnny's pride vs Roland's assumptions

jokes.text: if you know what I mean. - Yeah, you just told me what you mean.
jokes.setup: Roland being suggestive about his plans
jokes.punchline: Johnny pointing out Roland already explained everything
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback - Johnny's exasperation at Roland's redundant innuendo

jokes.text: If you catch my drift. - Yeah, I caught your drift when you told me what you mean.
jokes.setup: Roland continuing to be coy after explaining everything
jokes.punchline: Johnny's frustrated repetition of the same point
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the callback - Johnny's mounting frustration with Roland's redundancy

jokes.text: Oh really? She said, extra special. I think we should stay.
jokes.setup: David wanting to leave the graduation ceremony
jokes.punchline: Ted's innocent enthusiasm for the 'surprise' performance
jokes.explanation: Good setup for upcoming cringe - Ted's innocent optimism

jokes.text: A bold claim.
jokes.setup: David claiming the store is a success
jokes.punchline: Patrick's understated disagreement with David's assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Patrick understatement - gentle reality check for David's optimism

jokes.text: I've never done that before... with a guy.
jokes.setup: David and Patrick after their first kiss
jokes.punchline: David's vulnerable admission about his experience
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character moment - David's vulnerability and growth

jokes.text: Well, um, fortunately, I'm an very generous person.
jokes.setup: Patrick thanking David for making the kiss happen
jokes.punchline: David's characteristic self-aggrandizing response to intimacy
jokes.explanation: Perfect David response - deflecting vulnerability with humor while staying in character

jokes.text: Just preferably not before 10 a.m., 'cause I'm not really a morning person.
jokes.setup: David saying they can talk whenever Patrick wants
jokes.punchline: His immediate qualification about morning availability
jokes.explanation: Good David character moment - romantic availability with practical limitations

jokes.text: 'Happy day Alex and Davis'. - We've done the best we can.
jokes.setup: Reading the cake with wrong names
jokes.punchline: Moira's resignation about their parenting effort
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag about parental effort - wrong names sum up their general competence

jokes.text: Okay, I think that this is really pulling focus from my birthday a bit though.
jokes.setup: Family celebrating both graduation and birthday together
jokes.punchline: David's complaint about sharing attention at the dual celebration
jokes.explanation: Good callback to David's earlier resistance to sharing his special day
